qa_pairs.question: Is it possible for one process to interfere with the memory of another in an MPI program?
qa_pairs.answer: No, in MPI programs, it is not possible for one process to interfere with the memory of another.

qa_pairs.question: How are segmentation faults handled for debugging in MPI programs?
qa_pairs.answer: If an MPI program generates a segmentation fault, the resulting core file can be processed by standard serial debugging tools.

qa_pairs.question: Why might MPI programs seem more complex than programs using tools with implicit communication?
qa_pairs.answer: MPI programs may appear more complex than programs written with tools that support implicit communication due to the explicit management required for communication and synchronization.

qa_pairs.question: What should be minimized when designing an MPI program to achieve good parallel speed-up?
qa_pairs.answer: In designing an MPI program, one should take care to minimize communication overhead to achieve a good speed-up from the parallel computation.
